items.body: The video game giant owned by Microsoft plans to let 3,200 workers go. Will it be a big reset or a system crash?

items.hook: Psycho II is a 1982 horror novel by American writer Robert Bloch.
items.body: It is a sequel to his 1959 novel Psycho. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Mistress of the World is an eight-part 1919 silent film made in the Weimar Republic starring Mia May in the lead role.
items.body: The film, under the creative control of director Joe May, is noted for bringing together talent from across German cinema, including many who would work behind the camera during the emergence of German Expressionist cinema. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the rea

items.hook: The First House by Avni Doshi review – an intense portrait of marriage and freedom
items.body:
In the follow-up to the Booker-shortlisted Burnt Sugar, a woman seeks liberation from her controlling relationships

Avni Doshi’s second novel is narrated by an unnamed woman in the suburban US who is shocked to hear her husband ann

items.hook: UK crew unemployment rose as volume of film and TV releases fell 13% between 2022-2025
items.body: The production workforce was estimated to be working at about 50-55% capacity in 2025.

items.hook: Ittyanath Kunjikrishna Menon, better known as I.
items.body: M., was a Malayalam language writer from Kerala, India. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Day She Returns review – another Hong Sang-soo round of slow, reflective boozing really hits the spot
items.body:
No film-maker shows more day-drinking than Hong, and this study of an actor returning from a career break is the same again – as mysteriously addictive as ever

It will not surprise fans of the prolific lo-fi Korean master Hong Sang

items.hook: ‘Fun, propulsive, full of queer joy’: readers’ favourite albums of 2026 so far, from Muna to Raye and J Cole
items.body:
After the Guardian’s music critics chose their best of the half-year, we asked you for your picks – from Brian Jackson and Arlo Parks to Maya Hawke, Flea and more

items.hook: Gillian Armstrong remembers Sam Neill: ‘I can’t believe that the very next day he was gone’
items.body: The Australian director who gave the actor his big break in My Brilliant Career remembers ‘a smart, gentle, decent, passionate, loyal, surprisingly funny man’

items.hook: Memories is a 2013 Indian Malayalam-language slasher film written and directed by Jeethu Joseph.
items.body: The film stars Prithviraj Sukumaran, S. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Edward Harold Begbie, was an English journalist and the author of nearly 50 books and poems.
items.body: Besides studies of the Christian religion, he wrote numerous other books, including political satire, comedy, fiction, science fiction, plays and poetry. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Odyssey is a 'colossal piece of cinema', critics say in rave reviews
items.body: The film, starring Matt Damon and Zendaya, is director Sir Christopher Nolan's first since Oppenheimer.
